content: CHAPTER 1: CORPORATE IDENTITY & PHILOSOPHY 1.1 Origin Story & History Gift of Grace Food Manufacturing Corporation (GoGFMC) is a testament to the potential of local Filipino entrepreneurship. The company is a duly registered corporation and a proudly local brand headquartered in the heart of the Cordillera Administrative Region (CAR), specifically at #5 Purok 6, Pinsao Pilot Project, Baguio City,
source_file: Aragona et al. Gift of Grace MOA gemini ai.pdf
company: Gift of Grace Food Manufacturing Corporation

content: 2600, Benguet . The company's journey began in 2015/2017 (transitioning from a home-based endeavor to a formal business). It was born from a simple, domestic passion: a mother’s love for Korean delicacies. The co-founder, Janice Osenio Cadsi , a registered nurse by profession, began experimenting with kimchi recipes in her own kitchen.
source_file: Aragona et al. Gift of Grace MOA gemini ai.pdf
company: Gift of Grace Food Manufacturing Corporation

content: Her goal was to create a version of the traditional Korean dish that was tailored specifically to the Filipino palate—balancing the authentic spice and fermentation of Korea with the savory and slightly sweet notes preferred by Filipinos. What started as sharing homemade jars with neighbors quickly gained momentum. The product became a "neighborhood hit," and demand grew organically.
source_file: Aragona et al. Gift of Grace MOA gemini ai.pdf
company: Gift of Grace Food Manufacturing Corporation

content: Recognizing the market potential for healthy, flavorful, and locally produced food options, the founders formalized the business. Over the years, GoGFMC has evolved from a grassroots survival-level sole proprietorship into a budding enterprise and eventually a thriving corporation.
source_file: Aragona et al. Gift of Grace MOA gemini ai.pdf
company: Gift of Grace Food Manufacturing Corporation

content: By 2025, the company had successfully overcome early market roadblocks and secured partnerships with major trade institutions, catapulting it into a period of significant growth. Today, it stands as a model of resilience and innovation in Northern Luzon. 1.2 Mission, Vision, and Core Values The corporate philosophy of GoGFMC is deeply rooted in faith, community service, and sustainability.
source_file: Aragona et al. Gift of Grace MOA gemini ai.pdf
company: Gift of Grace Food Manufacturing Corporation

content: The name "Gift of Grace" itself reflects the founders' belief that their business is a vessel for sharing blessings. ● Mission Statement: The company’s mission transcends mere profitability. GoGFMC is dedicated to crafting healthy food products that promote physical well-being while simultaneously generating meaningful employment opportunities for the community.
source_file: Aragona et al. Gift of Grace MOA gemini ai.pdf
company: Gift of Grace Food Manufacturing Corporation

content: They believe that healthy food should nourish the body and uplift the spirit. A core tenet of their mission is to "extend blessings to our community through sustainable practices and job creation". ● Vision Statement: GoGFMC envisions transforming the food industry by producing products that not only taste great but also contribute to the greater good of the planet.
source_file: Aragona et al. Gift of Grace MOA gemini ai.pdf
company: Gift of Grace Food Manufacturing Corporation

content: ○ Specific Strategic Vision: To produce at least 10 different varieties of quality, healthy foods using locally-grown raw materials by the year 2025 . ○ Broader Vision: To empower local families and support a thriving local economy through sustainable manufacturing processes.
source_file: Aragona et al. Gift of Grace MOA gemini ai.pdf

content: ● Core Values: ○ Integrity: Upholding honesty and respect in all business dealings, ensuring products are safe and high-quality. ○ Inclusivity: Fostering a workplace and product line that caters to all, including the Muslim community through Halal certification. ○ Sustainability: Commitment to the circular economy, responsible sourcing, and waste reduction.
source_file: Aragona et al. Gift of Grace MOA gemini ai.pdf

source_file: Aragona et al. Gift of Grace MOA gemini ai.pdf

source_file: Aragona et al. Gift of Grace MOA gemini ai.pdf

source_file: Aragona et al. Gift of Grace MOA gemini ai.pdf

source_file: Aragona et al. Gift of Grace MOA gemini ai.pdf

content: Its flagship initiative is the "Social Inclusion through Bawang Peeling Program." ● Target Beneficiaries: Senior citizens, local housewives, and out-of-school youth. ● Activity: Providing paid work for peeling garlic (Bawang) used in production. This allows vulnerable community members to earn an income in a safe, accessible environment.

content: 5.2 Sustainability & Circular Economy The company actively practices Circular Economy principles. ● Upcycling: Repurposing biodegradable waste from vegetables (cabbage trimmings, peels) to prevent them from ending up in landfills.
source_file: Aragona et al. Gift of Grace MOA gemini ai.pdf

content: ● Responsible Sourcing: Raw materials (cabbage, rice, coffee) are sourced from local farmers in the Cordillera region, ensuring fair trade and reducing the carbon footprint associated with transport. 5.3 Educational Scholarships GoGFMC runs a scholarship program funded by a portion of its sales.
source_file: Aragona et al. Gift of Grace MOA gemini ai.pdf

content: CHAPTER 6: CONSUMER GUIDE & FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS This section compiles all consumer inquiries to serve as a reference for customer service and marketing. 6.1 Kimchi FAQs ● What is it? Fermented napa cabbage and radish with spices. ● Is it Vegan? Most varieties are, but check the label for shrimp paste/fish sauce. ● Is it Gluten-Free? Generally yes, but verify label for additives.
source_file: Aragona et al. Gift of Grace MOA gemini ai.pdf
company: Gift of Grace Food Manufacturing Corporation

content: ● How long does it last? Several months in the refrigerator. It becomes more sour (fermented) over time. ● Can I cook with it? Yes, excellent for stews, fried rice, and pancakes.
source_file: Aragona et al. Gift of Grace MOA gemini ai.pdf
company: Gift of Grace Food Manufacturing Corporation

content: ● Storage: Keep refrigerated in an airtight container to contain odors . 6.2 Tofu FAQs ● Is it Gluten-Free? Naturally yes. ● Storage: Keep refrigerated. Once opened, store in water and change the water daily. ● Shelf Life: Unopened lasts months; opened lasts 3-5 days. ● Can I freeze it? Yes. Freezing changes the texture to be more sponge-like, which is great for absorbing marinades.
source_file: Aragona et al. Gift of Grace MOA gemini ai.pdf
company: Gift of Grace Food Manufacturing Corporation

content: ● Do I need to cook it? It can be eaten raw (silken) but is usually cooked to enhance flavor . 6.3 Rice Coffee FAQs ● Does it have caffeine? No, it is 100% caffeine-free. ● Is it good for kids? Yes, safe for children and pregnant women. ● Can I add milk? Yes, prepare it exactly like traditional coffee with milk, sugar, or honey. ● Preparation: Steep in hot water or use a coffee press .
